findings.title: Unrestricted post-swap bridge entrypoints can exfiltrate assets already held by the gateway

best_agent_match.title: Any caller can invoke post-swap bridge entrypoints to exfiltrate residual gateway balances

findings.title: Optimism and Arbitrum routes trust attacker-chosen bridge contracts as spenders and call targets

best_agent_match.title: Optimism/Arbitrum routes let callers nominate arbitrary bridge spender contracts

findings.title: Hop routes forward gateway ETH to caller-chosen contracts

best_agent_match.title: Hop routes forward gateway ETH to caller-chosen contracts

findings.title: ZkSync composed bridge paths pull ERC20s from the user a second time and trust the wrong token field

best_agent_match.title: ZkSync post-swap bridging pulls tokens from the user a second time and trusts the wrong token field

findings.title: Many direct native bridge entrypoints can spend pre-existing gateway ETH without reconciling `msg.value`

best_agent_match.title: Direct native bridge entrypoints can spend pre-existing gateway ETH without matching `msg.value`

findings.title: `swapAndMultiBridge` is permanently unusable because the ratio-aggregation loop never increments

best_agent_match.title: Multi-bridge execution is permanently unusable because the ratio loop never increments

findings.title: Built-in routes below ID 385 cannot actually be disabled

best_agent_match.title: Built-in routes 0-384 cannot actually be disabled

rejected_candidates.title: All RouteIds 1-384 Map to Same Address - Suspicious Hardcoded Logic
rejected_candidates.reason: `addressAt()` is clearly generated deployment wiring for reserved routes, but this alone does not show an exploitable integrity break or fund-loss path.

rejected_candidates.title: Delegatecall Allows Arbitrary Code Execution in executeRoute
rejected_candidates.reason: `executeRoute` is the intended plugin dispatch mechanism; arbitrary execution only follows if the owner installs a malicious route, which is an admin trust assumption rather than a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Delegatecall in swapAndMultiBridge Allows Arbitrary Swap Execution
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the same intended route-dispatch architecture as `executeRoute`, not an independent bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Token Approvals via setApprovalForRouters
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-managed approvals to known routers are an explicit admin capability; the claim reduces to owner compromise risk.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on FeesTakerController Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: These controller methods are meant to be user-callable helpers and spend the caller's own approvals or value, not shared protocol funds.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted Refund Function in CelerImpl with No Access Control
rejected_candidates.reason: `refundCelerUser` validates the refund payload, requires the refund receiver to be the gateway, and forwards funds to the stored original user rather than the caller.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation in addRoute
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-only misconfiguration footgun with no realistic protocol-level exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Fallback Function Uses msg.sig for Route Routing
rejected_candidates.reason: The fallback router is an intentional dispatch mechanism; the report does not show an exploitable path beyond the already-reviewed route implementation bugs.

rejected_candidates.title: No Slippage Protection in Swap Implementations
rejected_candidates.reason: The 1inch calldata is generated off-chain and already encodes slippage/min-return constraints; this report does not identify a missing on-chain check beyond that design.

rejected_candidates.title: ERC20 Approvals Not Reset After Bridge Execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Persistent approvals to trusted external bridge routers are common integration behavior and the claim depends on later compromise of those trusted routers.

rejected_candidates.title: ExecuteControllers Allows Sequential Arbitrary Delegatecalls
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended controller-composition entrypoint, not a distinct vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: ExecuteRoutes Allows Sequential Arbitrary Delegatecalls
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended route-composition entrypoint, not a distinct vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation in addController
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-only configuration mistake, not a realistic adversarial exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: SwapAndBridge Uses Delegatecall Without Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: The bridge contracts are intentionally composing trusted swap routes through the gateway's route table; this is architectural, not a separate bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Rescue Functions Allow Draining All Funds
rejected_candidates.reason: These are explicit owner-only rescue powers and therefore part of the admin trust model.

rejected_candidates.title: Precision Loss in Bridge Ratio Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Integer rounding dust is minor and not a reportable protocol-risk issue compared with the real functional bug in the same flow.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value of Native Transfer in FeesTakerController
rejected_candidates.reason: The code checks the boolean result and reverts on failure; the candidate does not describe a real bug.
